channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India Plans DRAL Restructuring: New Private Partner Likely for Rafale Production and MRO Hub
item.description: India’s Ministry of Defence (MoD) is preparing to restructure the existing Dassault Reliance Aerospace Limited (DRAL) joint venture, a move that could significantly reshape the country’s future fighter aircraft manufacturing ecosystem. According to emerging inputs, the MoD is expected to push Dassault Aviation to onboard a new private-sector partner to replace the current minority stakeholder led by Anil Ambani.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Uttam AESA Radar Achieves Major Milestone: Extensive Flight Testing Completed on Tejas LSP-2 and LSP-3
item.description: India’s indigenous defence ecosystem has reached a pivotal achievement with the Uttam Active Electronically Scanned Array (AESA) radar, developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation’s Electronics and Radar Development Establishment (LRDE). This advanced fire control radar has undergone rigorous and extensive flight testing on two key Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas prototypes, LSP-2 and LSP-3, successfully validating its performance across critical air-to-air (A2A) and air-to-ground (A2G) modes. The completion of these trials underscores India’s growing capability to deliver world-class airborne sensors tailored for modern combat requirements.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: How S-400 Changed the Air War: PAF Shifted to Defensive Mode After Initial Clashes, Claims New Book
item.description: In the high-stakes aerial domain during Operation Sindoor in May 2025, the Indian Air Force’s deployment of the advanced S-400 Triumf surface-to-air missile system profoundly altered the battlefield dynamics. Following the intense initial engagements on May 7-8, Pakistan Air Force jets adopted a markedly cautious posture, steering clear of aggressive incursions once the true reach and lethality of India’s long-range SAMs became evident.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: UK, Japan May Offer India Observer Status in GCAP 6th-Gen Fighter Program, Full Partnership Unlikely
item.description: The United Kingdom and Japan are likely to offer India an observer position in the ambitious Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), a sixth-generation fighter jet initiative being jointly developed with Italy, according to sources cited by idrw.org. The move reflects growing interest in India’s participation, but stops short of granting full partnership due to concerns over programme timelines and integration complexities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ADSL Developing 30 km Loitering Munition System to Boost Indian Infantry’s Beyond-Line-of-Sight Strike Capability
item.description: Punjab-based Airbornics Defence and Space Private Limited (ADSL) is advancing India’s tactical strike ecosystem with the development of a portable loitering munition system designed to give small infantry units the ability to detect, track, and eliminate targets well beyond visual range. The system is engineered as a force-multiplier at the platoon and company level, enabling precision engagements at distances of up to 30 kilometers without reliance on larger artillery or air support assets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Advancing Air-Launched LRLACM Cruise Missile for Su-30MKI Deep Strike Capability
item.description: India’s long-range precision strike capability is set to expand significantly as development of the air-launched variant of the Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile (LRLACM) enters a crucial phase. According to sources familiar with the programme, integration work is underway to adapt the missile for launch from the Su-30MKI fighter aircraft, which will serve as the primary test platform during the initial trials.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy Opens MiG-29K Sustainment to Private Sector with Indigenous Development Push for Critical Systems
item.description: The Indian Navy’s continued investment in resolving the long-standing reliability and maintenance challenges of the MiG-29K fleet signals a clear strategic shift toward indigenized sustainment and lifecycle support. As the Navy works to improve serviceability rates and reduce dependence on foreign OEM support, it has begun actively engaging the domestic private sector to develop critical subsystems for the carrier-based fighter. This move aligns with broader Atmanirbhar Bharat objectives while addressing operational gaps that have affected the MiG-29K’s deployment readiness.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Forget Kolkata Your Military Could barely reach Jammu, Omar Abdullah mocks Pakistani Defence Minister
item.description: Reminding Pakistan of Operation Sindoor, Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah on Tuesday said that it would be in Islamabad’s interest to focus on improving relations with India rather than engaging in rhetoric and escalating tensions. He also welcomed the busting of a Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) terror module by Srinagar Police, but stressed the need to strengthen surveillance and plug any remaining loopholes in the security framework.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: System development cycle shrinking, material development needs to keep pace: DRDO chief Kamat
item.description: Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) chief Samir V Kamat on Tuesday said the biggest challenge in the material development domain is that its cycle takes 10-15 years, while the system development cycle is “continuously shrinking”.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Attainment Of Criticality At Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor Reflects India’s Indigenous Strength
item.description: The attainment of criticality at the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) marks more than a technological milestone; it reflects the maturity of India’s long standing nuclear vision and the strength of its indigenous capabilities, an official statement said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Expert Calls Kalpakkam Nuclear Reactor Attaining Criticality As ‘Step Towards Atmanirbhar Bharat’
item.description: Nuclear energy expert Dr Neelam Goyal on Tuesday called the attainment of criticality at the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) in Tamil Nadu’s Kalpakkam, as a “big win for India” and an “important step towards ‘Atmanirbhar Bharat’.”
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Cross-border drug smuggling a major challenge for border states: CM Omar Abdullah
item.description: Chief Minister Omar Abdullah on Tuesday said drug smuggling from across the border remains a major challenge, not limited to Jammu and Kashmir, but faced by all border states across the country.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 2 dead, 20 injured in firing after mob storms CRPF camp in Manipur
item.description: At least two persons were killed and 20 others injured in firing by security forces after hundreds of people stormed a CRPF camp in Manipur’s Bishnupur district and burnt vehicles during a protest on Tuesday, the police said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 15 Gelatin Sticks, Over 50 Detonators: Cache Of Explosives Recovered In Nagpur’s Residential Area
item.description: A large cache of explosives was recovered from a residential locality near CA Road in Nagpur on Tuesday, raising serious security concerns in one of the city’s most sensitive zones, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IndiGo aircraft hit by unmanned vehicle at Kolkata airport, plane grounded
item.description: An unmanned third-party vehicle on the apron hit a parked IndiGo aircraft at Kolkata airport on Tuesday, prompting the airline to ground the plane for inspection, the carrier said.
item.dc:creator: admin
